issues.display_title: Issue #4095: Stitching Clear All Uploaded Images
issues.main_description_html:
Stitching Clear All Uploaded Images

Date: 2026-06-24
Status: Approved

Summary

Add a Clear All Uploaded Images action to the Auto Stitching upload page so users can remove all uploaded stitching images at once before payment.

Context

Users sometimes finish a stitching job and later need to prepare a new stitching set for the same tour. At the moment, old uploaded stitching images can remain visible on the Auto Stitching page. If the user needs to replace the set, they have to delete each image one by one.

This creates extra work when there are many images. In the example provided, the page shows 99 uploaded images. Removing them individually is slow and frustrating.

The action should only be available before the user pays for stitching. Once the user has paid, the uploaded stitching images should be protected and the clear-all option should not be available.

Goal

Give unpaid users a simple way to remove every uploaded stitching image from the current tour in one action.

User Experience

When the user is on the Auto Stitching upload page and has uploaded stitching images, they should see a Clear All Uploaded Images action.

Clicking the action should show a confirmation message before anything is deleted. The message should make it clear that all uploaded stitching images for this tour will be removed and that the user can upload a fresh set afterward.

If the user confirms, all uploaded stitching images are removed from the page. The uploaded image count should return to zero, and the empty upload area should appear again so the user can start over.

If the user cancels, nothing changes.

Delete Action

When the user confirms the Clear All Uploaded Images action, the app should send one delete request for the current tour's uploaded stitching images.

Request:

DELETE {base_url}/stitching/images

Example request body:

{
  "moderation_id": 3561007,
  "file_names": ["Stitch (5).jpg", "Stitch (6).jpg", "Stitch (7).jpg"]
}

The moderation_id should come from the current tour. The file_names list should include every uploaded stitching image shown on the page at the time the user confirms the clear/delete action.

Availability Rules

The Clear All Uploaded Images action should be available only when:

  • The user is on the Auto Stitching upload page.
  • The tour has uploaded stitching images.
  • No stitching upload is currently in progress.
  • The user has not paid for stitching yet.

The action should not be available when:

  • The user has already paid for stitching.
  • Images are currently uploading.
  • There are no uploaded stitching images to clear.

Confirmation

The action should require confirmation because it removes many images at once.

Suggested confirmation meaning:

Are you sure you want to delete all uploaded stitching images? You can upload a new set afterward.

The confirmation should have a clear cancel option and a clear delete/confirm option.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Users can clear all uploaded stitching images in one action before payment.
  2. The action requires confirmation before deleting anything.
  3. Canceling the confirmation leaves all images unchanged.
  4. Confirming sends DELETE {base_url}/stitching/images with the current moderation_id and all uploaded stitching image file names.
  5. After clearing, the uploaded image count shows zero.
  6. After clearing, the page returns to the empty upload state.
  7. The action is hidden or disabled after the user has paid for stitching.
  8. The action is hidden or disabled while images are uploading.
  9. Existing one-by-one image delete behavior continues to work before payment.
  10. No payment, stitch purchase, or upload speed behavior changes.

Out of Scope

  • Clearing images after payment.
  • Changing the stitch payment flow.
  • Changing image upload sources.
  • Changing upload speed controls.
  • Changing how single-image deletion works.
  • Adding automatic cleanup after a job finishes.

Relevant Backend Tasks:

GoThruMedia/GoThru-API-New#1432

Demo by Developer

2026-06-24.15-58-03.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4092: ZIP Download Direct File Link
issues.main_description_html:
ZIP Download Direct File Link

Summary

Change the ZIP Download action in Auto Stitch Review so it opens the actual ZIP file directly, instead of sending staff through the current download helper link.

Context

Support staff use the Auto Stitch Review page to inspect stitching jobs and download ZIP files from the action menu. Today, the ZIP Download button opens a link like:

https://api.gothru.co/get-zipfile?user_id=1275&moderation_id=3561423

This is not the link the team expects. Staff need the button to open the file directly, matching the format shared in the support request:

https://gothru.co/moderation/1275/stitching/3561423.zip

The page already shows the needed user ID and moderation ID for each stitching row, so the button can use those values to point to the right file.

Goal

When a staff member clicks ZIP Download, the browser opens the direct ZIP file link:

https://gothru.co/moderation/{userid}/stitching/{moderation_id}.zip

For example, if the user ID is 1275 and the moderation ID is 3561423, the opened file should be:

https://gothru.co/moderation/1275/stitching/3561423.zip

User Experience

The support staff workflow should feel unchanged. Staff still open the same action menu and click the same ZIP Download option. The only difference is the destination: the download should now come from the direct file URL.

There should be no new screen, confirmation step, or extra message. The action should continue to open in a new browser tab or download window as it does today.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The ZIP Download option remains in the same action menu.
  2. Clicking ZIP Download opens a direct ZIP file link using the selected row's user ID and moderation ID.
  3. The link format is https://gothru.co/moderation/{userid}/stitching/{moderation_id}.zip.
  4. The old helper link format is no longer used for this button.
  5. Other actions in the same menu, such as Reset Job and Send Email, continue to work as before.

Out of Scope

  • Changing the button label or menu design.
  • Changing how stitching jobs are listed.
  • Changing email behavior.
  • Changing reset job behavior.
  • Adding new error messages or download status indicators.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-24.15-50-13.mp4
